mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in unemployment rate above 8.5% signals deteriorating labor market conditions, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days due to reduced consumer spending expectations.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 8.5% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in housing starts (HOUST) and consumer spending (RSAFS) indicate weak demand conditions, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between combined HOUST/RSAFS signals and SPX returns falls below 0.05 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in inflation (CPIAUCSL) above 2.5% combined with stable federal funds rates signals inflationary pressures, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as central banks tighten policy.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 50% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5% and FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below 93.0 signals weakening manufacturing activity, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days due to reduced corporate earnings expectations.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where INDPRO falls below 93.0.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) above 150k signals strong labor market conditions, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days due to elevated consumer confidence.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 50% of instances where PAYEMS exceeds 150k.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly decline in GDP (GDPC1) below 13,300 billion signals economic contraction, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as risk appetite declines.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 50% of instances where GDPC1 falls below 13300.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in inflation (CPIAUCSL) above 2.0% combined with rising federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS > 0.25%) signals aggressive monetary tightening, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between combined CPIAUCSL/FEDFUNDS signals and SPX returns falls below 0.05 over any 3-year window.
